# Oracle provenance for `sightglass-strong` goldens

All `.stdout.expected` / `.stderr.expected` files in this directory were
generated by running the `.wasm` kernels under an **independent** runtime so
that the WasmEdge harness in `test/ir/ir_benchmark_test.cpp` has a
non-tautological correctness check: a regression in the WasmEdge loader,
interpreter, LLVM JIT, or IR JIT path will show up as a golden mismatch
rather than silently corrupting both the runtime output and the oracle.

## Primary oracle

- **wasmtime 43.0.1** (`cd4b6ed9b`, 2026-04-09), installed at `~/.wasmtime/bin/wasmtime`.
- Bench import shim: `bench_stub.wat` in this directory. It exports two
  `() -> ()` no-op functions (`start`, `end`) and is attached to the wasm
  under test via `--preload bench=bench_stub.wat`.
- Each kernel was run from a per-kernel sandbox directory so that WASI
  preopens expose the required input file(s) at the paths the kernel's
  source reads (e.g. `default.input`, `default.input.md`, `secret.der`,
  `shootout-ackermann.{m,n}.input`). The sandbox also contains `bench_stub.wat`.
- Invocation (per kernel):
  ```sh
  wasmtime run \
    --preload bench=bench_stub.wat \
    --dir .::/ \
    $kernel.wasm \
    >$kernel.stdout.expected \
    2>$kernel.stderr.expected
  ```

## Cross-checks

None performed in the first pass. If a strengthened kernel is later
found to have platform-dependent output, add a note here and consider a
second-oracle diff against `benchmarks/build-native.sh`.

## Platform-dependent kernels

(none recorded yet)
